titleStmt.title: SII 20.81: original edition by G. S. Gai
No. 81. (B.K. No. 15 of 1935-36.) LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

summary.p: This badly damaged record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 4[7], Plava, Phālguṇa, Amāvāsyā, Sunday, Saṁkramaṇa, solar eclipse. It corresponds to A.D. 1122, March 10. The weekday was, however, Friday and no Saṁkramaṇa occurred on that day.
The inscription records a gift of land to god Sōmēśvara by Daṇḍanāyaka Nāgavarmmayya after purchasing it from the 120 mahājanas of Puligere. One sēnāpati Sōvidēva is mentioned.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbicaṁdracāmaracāravē traiḷōkyanagarāraṁbhamūḷastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē .
śrīvallabhalalāmanāhavamallabhūbhṛtsūnu vikramādityamahīvallabhanesedaṁ tribhuvana mallaṁ
mahāmātyaṁ sēnāpati sōvidēvanādēśi salurvvīnuta nā
nāgavarmmacamūpa śrīramaṇī ramaṇaṁ siriramaṇapadā
sāgavarmmadaṁḍādhīśa svasti śrīma
ccāḷukya vikramakālada 47 neya plava saṁvatsarada phālguṇadamāvāsye Ādivāra saṁkrama
ṇa vyatīpāta sūryyagrahaṇadandu dhareyellaṁ tanantapāḷa(ḷa)camū vallabhaṁ
kamāhēśvara purikarapuradoḷāṟagoṁḍaḍe sōmēśvara dēvābhyarccanakā
daradoḷama hannirmmatara holadoḷagolditta nācaṁdratāraṁ .
© śrī rājadhānipaṭṭaṇaṁ puligeṟeya lliya mahājana nūṟippa diṁbara kayyalu
daṁḍanāyakaṁ nāgavarmmayyaṁgaḷu dravyanivēdanaṁgeydu dhārāpūrvvakaṁ sarvvana maśya sarvvabā
dhāparihāramāge paḍedu sōmēśvaradēvara koṭṭa bhūmiya pramā
ṇa vūriṁ baḍagaṇa Adakke sīme mūḍaleḷa
tatiya riya ha bidigana koḷa pārvvara pola mēre paḍuvalu
parvvatada mūḍaṇa sīmeya liṁgada kallu goggiya basadiya kiṁnarivola
teṁkaṇa sīme mēre . sāmānyōyaṁ dharmmasēturnṛpāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ .
sarvvānētānbhāvinaḻpārtthivēndrānbhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ . svadattaṁ para
dattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ . saṣṭirvvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ .
Digital edition of SII 20.81 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
